files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 2736c39..67dd7b7 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,33 +3,34 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
 - Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent. The rationale string in next-week is the weekly log; do not call nonexistent log function.
-- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`. Always respond in the same week. Last sender matters; if last sender is agent, wait for customer.
+- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`. Last sender matters; if last sender is agent, wait for customer. Some timed-out enterprise threads may remain open even after subscription status `lost`.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshot replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` accepts `targeted_spend={...}`; keyword `by_group=` errors. Use `targeted_spend={}` to clear.
+- `set_targeted_ops_spend` works with `targeted_spend={...}` for group spend. Use `targeted_spend={}` to clear.
 - In-app ads strength stays 0; quality/reputation > small ad revenue. `set_prices` reprices only new subscription rows, not existing active rows immediately.
 
-## State at Day 70 before Week 10 actions
-- Cash $567,286; 21,651 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats. Open issues 1,919 but almost all age0 (S2 1,793 age0 + 1 age6; S3 126 age0 + 1 age10). No outage/overload; peak usage 3.24M/day vs tier4 cap 8M; weekly p95 peak 275ms.
-- Week64-70 economics: sub cash $544.5k; ads $155.4k; ops $59.5k; dev $38.5k; capacity $28.0k; compute $99.5k; lead cost $9.9k => cash +$153.8k.
-- S2 content funnel remained excellent overall: 17,873 paid S2 content leads on $140k spend, 12,098 subscribers (~67.7% CVR). Day70 CVR fell to ~45.7% after competitor posts (+0.09/+0.10 quality pressure) and quota complaints, so watch whether new quality/dev/post restores it.
-- Current active MRR by rows ≈ $1.43M/mo (mostly S2 A at old/new prices). Upcoming existing renewal wave: small days71-86, then larger days87-98, especially day94-98 from week9 cohorts.
-- D_E09 enterprise lead #22814 (177 seats, Real Estate Groups) arrived day65. Replied day70 with A $49 / B $72 / C $99 month-to-month; last sender is agent, thread still open. D_E09 LinkedIn spend produced 0 direct ad leads during week9, so cut it.
+## State at Day 77 before Week 11 actions
+- Cash $663,165; 37,260 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats; active MRR by rows about $2.70M/mo (S2 A dominates: 34.4k S2 A avg future price $70.81, 792 S2 B avg $148; S3 2.1k total).
+- Week 71-77 economics: subscription cash $669.1k; ads $186.9k; ops $71.8k; dev $68.3k; capacity $28.0k; compute $206.0k; lead cost $12.3k => cash +$95.9k.
+- S2 content funnel still excellent: 21,591 S2 content leads on $175k, 13,853 subscribers (64.2% CVR), first cash $563.2k (avg $40.66). S3 ads also profitable at small scale. Network added 1,220 S2 and 99 S3 subs.
+- Usage rose to 5.99M on day77 vs tier4 cap 8M (week avg 4.74M); no overload/outage, p95 peak 254ms. Open issues 3,364 but almost all age0 (S2 3,156 age0 plus one each age1/3/13; S3 207 age0 plus one age17). Ops resolved prior-day issue waves but volume is rising.
+- Social day77: quota friction persists (S2/S3), plus S2 complaints about support handoffs and quality consistency. Competitor posts day71-72 reported ~0.095 and two ~0.077 quality bumps. Day70 quota reply was positive (17.8k views, +0.72 S2/S3/E groups).
+- Enterprise: D_E09 lead #22814 timed out/lost day72 despite day70 agent offer; thread still open with last sender agent but subscription status is lost. Do not spend on D_E09 for now unless a fresh lead/inbox appears.
 
-## Week 10 actions made for Days 71-77
-- Pricing for new cohorts: A/B/C = $79/$159/$279; model tiers A3/B4/C5; quotas raised to A=5k/day, B=50k/day, C=200k/day.
-- Lead promos adjusted to preserve first-month A acquisition prices despite higher list: S2=$40 (A first $39), S3=$45 (A first $34), all others 0. No recurring promos. Ads strength 0.
-- Ads: content_marketing S2 $25,000/day (modest scale of proven funnel), content_marketing S3 $1,000/day, social_media S3 $700/day; LinkedIn/search/referral cleared. Total ads $26.7k/day.
-- Service/product: capacity remains tier4 (8M/day cap); global ops $8,000/day + targeted ops S2 $1,500/day and S3 $750/day. Global dev $1,000/day + targeted dev S2 $4,000/day, S3 $4,000/day, D_E09 $750/day.
-- Posted day70 reply to S2 quota post 143 (agent_post_id 11): announced Starter 5k/day, Pro 50k, Team 200k with warnings/checkpoint/resume/auto-reviewed burst windows.
+## Week 11 actions made for Days 78-84
+- Pricing for new cohorts raised to A/B/C = $89/$199/$399; model tiers remain A3/B4/C5. Lead promos changed to S2=$50 and S3=$55, preserving first-month A prices ($39 S2, $34 S3) while lifting future renewal ARPU; no recurring promotions.
+- Quotas increased to A=10k/day, B=150k/day, C=500k/day to address burst-work complaints. Public post day77 (agent_post_id 12) announced the quota/capacity/support/eval changes.
+- Capacity moved to tier5 (25M/day, $10k/day) because day77 usage was already ~6M and continued acquisition would likely exceed tier4.
+- Ads kept stable (cash-managed): content_marketing S2 $25k/day, content_marketing S3 $1k/day, social_media S3 $700/day; all other ad channels cleared. Ads strength remains 0.
+- Spend increased for reliability/quality: global ops $10k/day + targeted ops S2 $2k/day, S3 $1k/day. Global dev $5k/day + targeted dev S2 $5k/day, S3 $5k/day; D_E09 targeted dev cleared. No R&D started yet.
 
-## Strategy / watch on Day 77
-- This week is a cash-managed scale test: higher recurring list prices for new cohorts while preserving first-month conversion, plus moderate S2 ad scale and more persistent quality/support spend.
-- First checks Day77: S2 content leads/CVR/first-cash payback; whether day70 CVR drop persists; compute and daily usage vs 8M cap; any overload/outage; open issue ages >1 day; social reaction to post 11; D_E09 response/counter/acceptance.
-- If peak usage >6M/day, p95/error worsens, or any overload occurs, move to tier5. If support ages >1 day, raise ops/targeted ops before more ads.
-- If S2 content CVR stays <45% or cash <450k, cut S2 ads back to <=$15k/day and reassess promo/quality. If cash >700k, CVR >60%, no aged issues, and usage <6M, continue/consider modest scale.
-- Delay big R&D until cash >$1M after renewal wave; targeted dev is still more cost-effective for S2/S3. Consider tier2+tier3 R&D once cash cushion remains safe.
+## Strategy / watch on Day 84
+- Watch cash after higher capacity/dev/ops and quota-driven compute. If cash <450k or S2 CVR falls <45%, cut S2 content spend to <=$15k/day and reassess price/promo/quality.
+- Watch usage vs tier5: if peak >20M/day or latency/errors worsen, prepare tier6; if usage stays <10M and cash tight, tier5 can remain but do not scale ads.
+- Watch issue ages: any meaningful S2/S3 issues >1 day means raise ops/targeted ops before more ads. Check response to post 12.
+- Validate A=$89 future list via lead CVR now and renewal churn later (day108+ for new cohorts). If new lead CVR drops despite same first-month price, roll list back or increase dev/post; if renewals at $69/$79 hold on days94-107, consider keeping/raising.
+- Delay big R&D until cash safely >$1M after renewal wave; targeted S2/S3 dev remains the highest ROI. Consider tier2/tier3 R&D once cushion is safe.
 
-## Forecasts submitted from Day 70 (for calibration)
-- Day77: point ~$635k, 95% CI $400k-$850k.
-- Day98: point ~$1.20M, 95% CI $400k-$2.30M.
-- Day154: point ~$5.5M, 95% CI $1.2M-$12M.
-- Day252: point ~$24M, 95% CI $4M-$60M.
+## Forecasts to be submitted from Day 77
+- Day84: point ~$600k, 95% CI $400k-$850k.
+- Day105: point ~$1.55M, 95% CI $0.60M-$2.80M.
+- Day161: point ~$9.0M, 95% CI $1.5M-$22M.
+- Day259: point ~$32M, 95% CI $4M-$85M.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json
results = {}
# Pricing: modestly lift future ARPU while preserving first-month entry prices via targeted lead promos.
results['set_prices'] = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=89, B=199, C=399)
results['set_model_tiers'] = nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5)
results['set_usage_quotas'] = nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=10000, B=150000, C=500000)
# No recurring promotions for core S2/S3; use lead promos only to keep acquisition hurdle low.
results['set_promotion'] = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
results['set_lead_promotion'] = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(
    global_promotion=0.0,
    by_group={"S1": 0.0, "S2": 50.0, "S3": 55.0, "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0},
    by_channel={},
    by_channel_group={}
)
results['set_ads_strength'] = nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})
# Keep proven acquisition budget stable while service/product catch up.
results['set_targeted_ad_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 25000.0, "S3": 1000.0},
    "social_media": {"S3": 700.0},
    "linkedin": {},
    "search_ads": {},
    "referral_program": {}
})
# Scale capacity ahead of next week's likely >8M/day usage.
results['set_capacity_tier'] = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5)
# Increase support and quality investment; remove stale D_E09 dev now that that lead is lost.
results['set_daily_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=10000.0, development=5000.0)
results['set_targeted_ops_spend'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 2000.0, "S3": 1000.0})
results['set_targeted_dev_spend'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 5000.0, "S3": 5000.0})
# Public response to quota/support/consistency concerns.
try:
    results['post_social_media'] = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=(
        "Update: we hear the burst + support feedback. Today Starter=10k/day, Pro=150k, Team=500k; "
        "we've scaled capacity, added ticket continuity notes so no re-explaining, and expanded regression evals to stabilize answers. Keep calling out misses."
    ))
except Exception as e:
    results['post_social_media_error'] = str(e)
print(json.dumps(results, indent=2, default=str))

import novamind_api as nm, json, math

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql)['rows']
    except Exception as e:
        return [{'ERROR': str(e), 'SQL': sql}]

def pr(title, obj, maxchars=6000):
    print('\n===', title, '===')
    s=json.dumps(obj, indent=2, default=str)
    print(s[:maxchars])
    if len(s)>maxchars: print('...TRUNCATED', len(s))

print('current_day', getattr(nm.vars, 'current_day', None))
# current cash from ledger
pr('cash ledger sum', q("SELECT SUM(amount) AS cash FROM ledger"))
pr('ledger by category days 71-77', q("SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amount FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC"))
pr('ledger by day/category days 71-77', q("SELECT day, category, SUM(amount) amount FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category"), 12000)
pr('daily net cash 64-77', q("SELECT day, SUM(amount) net FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN 64 AND 77 GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"))
pr('subs active by group plan price', q("SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan, price"), 20000)
pr('active subs by group/plan aggregate', q("SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan"), 12000)
pr('active sub cohort by start_day group last 35 days', q("SELECT s.start_day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.start_day>=43 GROUP BY s.start_day, c.group_id ORDER BY s.start_day DESC, c.group_id"), 20000)
# cohort performance all customers created last week by source/group: total, subscribed, lost statuses
pr('customers created days 71-77 by group/source/status', q("SELECT c.created_day, c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status ORDER BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status"), 30000)
pr('lead conversion created days 71-77 by group/source', q("SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, COUNT(*) leads, SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) subscribed, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),1) cvr, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN s.effective_price ELSE NULL END),2) avg_eff FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source"), 20000)
pr('ad leads table days 71-77', q("SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, spend, leads_generated, ROUND(CASE WHEN spend>0 THEN leads_generated*1000.0/spend ELSE NULL END,2) leads_per_1k FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 ORDER BY day,channel_id,group_id"), 20000)
pr('ad performance agg days 71-77', q("SELECT a.channel_id, a.group_id, SUM(a.spend) spend, SUM(a.leads_generated) ad_leads, ROUND(SUM(a.leads_generated)*1000.0/SUM(a.spend),2) leads_per_1k, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 AND c.group_id=a.group_id AND c.acquisition_source=a.channel_id) created, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 AND c.group_id=a.group_id AND c.acquisition_source=a.channel_id AND s.status='subscribed') subs FROM ad_channel_leads a WHERE a.day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY a.channel_id,a.group_id ORDER BY spend DESC"), 12000)
pr('issues open by group age', q("SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id, days_open ORDER BY group_id, days_open"), 12000)
pr('issues opened/resolved days 71-77 by group', q("SELECT group_id, open_day, COUNT(*) opened, SUM(CASE WHEN status='resolved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) already_resolved FROM issues WHERE open_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY group_id,open_day ORDER BY open_day,group_id"), 20000)
pr('issues resolved days 71-77 by group', q("SELECT group_id, resolved_day, COUNT(*) resolved FROM issues WHERE resolved_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY group_id,resolved_day ORDER BY resolved_day,group_id"), 12000)
pr('service days 64-77', q("SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day BETWEEN 64 AND 77 ORDER BY day"), 20000)
pr('usage by group days 71-77', q("SELECT u.day, c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT u.customer_id) users, SUM(u.usage_units) usage FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id WHERE u.day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY u.day,c.group_id ORDER BY u.day,c.group_id"), 20000)
# upcoming billings based on active subs billing day mod; current day 77 => days 78-105? bill if day%30 mod matches
pr('upcoming billings active next 35 days by due_day/group/plan', q("WITH days(d) AS (SELECT 78 UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<112) SELECT days.d AS due_day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) cash_due FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.status='subscribed' AND (days.d % 30)=s.billing_day_mod30 JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id GROUP BY days.d,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY days.d,c.group_id,s.plan"), 30000)
pr('upcoming billings active next 35 by day', q("WITH days(d) AS (SELECT 78 UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<112) SELECT days.d AS due_day, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) cash_due FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.status='subscribed' AND (days.d % 30)=s.billing_day_mod30 GROUP BY days.d ORDER BY days.d"), 12000)
pr('cancellations days 71-77 by group/plan/start', q("SELECT s.end_day, c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 GROUP BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day ORDER BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day"), 12000)
pr('open enterprise threads', q("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 ORDER BY customer_id, day, turn_number"), 30000)
pr('enterprise last by open thread', q("WITH m AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT t.* FROM enterprise_turns t JOIN m ON m.mid=t.message_id ORDER BY t.day,t.customer_id"), 20000)
pr('notifications 64-77', q("SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE day BETWEEN 64 AND 77 ORDER BY day,notification_id"), 12000)
pr('agent social posts', q("SELECT * FROM agent_social_media_posts ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 20"), 10000)
pr('recent public posts table', q("SELECT * FROM social_media_posts WHERE day BETWEEN 71 AND 77 ORDER BY day DESC, post_id DESC LIMIT 100"), 30000)
pr('config overrides recent', q("SELECT * FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20"), 20000)
pr('research projects table', q("SELECT * FROM research_projects ORDER BY started_day DESC, tier"), 12000)
try:
    pr('cost_info', nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), 20000)
except Exception as e: print('cost_info error', e)
try:
    pr('market_overview', nm.market.get_market_overview(), 20000)
except Exception as e: print('market_overview error', e)
try:
    pr('research list', nm.research.list_research_projects(), 20000)
except Exception as e: print('research list error', e)
try:
    pr('social api', nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=80), 30000)
except Exception as e: print('social api error', e)

import novamind_api as nm, re, collections, json
# Query subscription payments days 71-77 (all 16k rows ok >5k? query limit 5000! need per day)
agg=collections.defaultdict(lambda: {'n':0,'amount':0})
for d in range(71,78):
    rows=nm.query(f"SELECT amount,note FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day={d}")['rows']
    ids=[]
    for r in rows:
        m=re.search(r'customer (\d+)', r['note'] or '')
        if m: ids.append((int(m.group(1)), r['amount']))
    # in chunks query customers
    for i in range(0,len(ids),800):
        chunk=ids[i:i+800]
        idlist=','.join(str(cid) for cid,amt in chunk)
        cmap={row['customer_id']:row for row in nm.query(f"SELECT customer_id,group_id,acquisition_source,created_day FROM customers WHERE customer_id IN ({idlist})")['rows']}
        for cid,amt in chunk:
            c=cmap.get(cid,{})
            key=(d,c.get('created_day'),c.get('group_id'),c.get('acquisition_source'),amt)
            agg[key]['n']+=1; agg[key]['amount']+=amt
# print grouped by created_day same as payment day vs renewals
sumagg=collections.defaultdict(lambda:{'n':0,'amount':0})
for (payday,created,group,src,amt),v in agg.items():
    cohort='new' if created==payday else 'renewal_or_other'
    key=(payday,cohort,group,src)
    sumagg[key]['n']+=v['n']; sumagg[key]['amount']+=v['amount']
out=[]
for key,v in sumagg.items(): out.append({'payday':key[0],'cohort':key[1],'group':key[2],'source':key[3],'n':v['n'],'amount':v['amount'],'avg':v['amount']/v['n']})
out.sort(key=lambda x:(x['payday'],x['cohort'],str(x['group']),str(x['source'])))
print(json.dumps(out,indent=2)[:30000])
# overall by cohort group source
sum2=collections.defaultdict(lambda:{'n':0,'amount':0})
for rec in out:
 key=(rec['cohort'],rec['group'],rec['source']); sum2[key]['n']+=rec['n']; sum2[key]['amount']+=rec['amount']
out2=[]
for key,v in sum2.items(): out2.append({'cohort':key[0],'group':key[1],'source':key[2],'n':v['n'],'amount':v['amount'],'avg':v['amount']/v['n']})
out2.sort(key=lambda x:(x['cohort'],str(x['group']),str(x['source'])))
print('---OVERALL')
print(json.dumps(out2,indent=2)[:20000])

import novamind_api as nm, json
queries=[
('cust22814','SELECT * FROM customers WHERE customer_id=22814'),
('subs22814','SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE customer_id=22814'),
('large cust recent','SELECT c.customer_id,c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,c.email,c.company_size_descriptor,c.company_primary_concern,s.status,s.plan,s.effective_price,s.seat_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.customer_type="large" ORDER BY c.created_day DESC LIMIT 80')]
for name,sql in queries:
 print('---', name)
 print(json.dumps(nm.query(sql)['rows'], indent=2, default=str)[:12000])
